data.title: Women-Founded Baloo Living Hits 100,000 Orders with Sustainable Weighted Blankets
data.description: On March 27, 2026, Baloo Living, a wellness brand founded by women and known for its sustainable and chemical-free weighted blankets, celebrated a significant achievement by completing its 100,000th customer order. This accomplishment arrives amidst forecasts indicating a substantial expansion in the global weighted blanket sector, with an anticipated increase from a market value of $1.01 million.

data.title: Translation error "Dài Bì" caused chaos in China's blockchain industry. Danny Deng warns against using it for "Token."
data.description: The translation of "token" as "Dài Bì" (代币 - Currency Substitute) is causing catastrophic consequences, according to Danny Deng, an Associate Research Fellow at The Hong Kong Polytechnic University. Deng, who is also the Co-Director of the AI FinTech Trading Research Group, highlights the significant impact of this mistranslation in the financial sector. The issue affects various stakeholders due to the confusion surrounding the term, emphasizing the need for accurate translations in these contexts.

data.title: 80% of ‘Stretch’ buyers are small investors; Michael Saylor says they invest in Bitcoin for the long term to avoid volatility.
data.description: Michael Saylor discussed how Stretch shares serve as a pathway for individuals who have faith in the long-term future of Bitcoin but struggle with its short-term unpredictability. This investment option offers a more stable approach for those who believe in the lasting presence of Bitcoin, catering to individuals who cannot withstand the current volatility in the market.

data.title: David Sacks completes 130-day stint as Trump's crypto and AI czar, to head new tech advisory group with key leaders.
data.description: David Sacks has been appointed to head a newly formed technology-focused advisory group by the White House. The group will feature prominent tech figures such as Jensen Huang from Nvidia and Mark Zuckerberg from Meta.

data.title: Zenas BioPharma Announces Pricing of Concurrent Public Offerings of 2.50% Convertible Senior Notes Due 2032 and Common Stock with Aggregate Gross Proceeds of $300.0 Million
data.description: Zenas BioPharma, Inc., a global biopharmaceutical company based in Waltham, Massachusetts, has declared the pricing of its recent public offerings. The company revealed the pricing of $200.0 million aggregate principal amount of its 2.50% convertible senior notes due 2032 as part of its Convertible Notes Offering. Additionally, Zenas BioPharma announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 5,000,000 shares of its common stock at $20.00 per share in its Equity Offering. The company is focused on advancing transformative therapies for individuals with autoimmune diseases and aims to emerge as a leader in this field.

data.title: New study: Active Protection Mechanisms in Buy Programs set new exit rules based on trend state instead of profit-and-loss figures. More details in the source.
data.description: The research paper introduces a new perspective on stop-loss strategies, categorizing them into passive and active approaches. It advocates for the use of trend state as a basis for exit rules rather than focusing solely on profit or loss numbers. The study outlines precise protective exit criteria for the identified buy points derived earlier. This shift in approach aims to enhance risk management strategies in trading scenarios.

data.title: London theater tickets are 50% cheaper than Broadway. Enjoy "Paddington" for less and grab a marmalade snack!
data.description: For the cost of a ticket to a Broadway show, you can enjoy "Paddington" in the West End at half the price. However, tickets are in high demand. Additionally, you can indulge in a marmalade sandwich during the show.
